title: 2022 Apr 17 Noliberalism and Mindfulness
justification: This talk directly and comprehensively addresses the chapter theme of 'Neoliberalism and the Dharma,' offering a clear structural arc from defining ideology and neoliberalism's core attitudes (greed, competition) through its social consequences (race to the bottom, erosion of community), to the Buddhist critique (Mara's work, co-optation of mindfulness), and finally to the Dharmic response (generosity, community, compassion). It features memorable examples (Gordon Gekko quote, Uber bidding, P&O sackings), the powerful Buddha quote on giving and sharing, and concludes with a call to shift from 'me to we'—providing excellent literary potential and a strong spine for grafting complementary material.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma systematically critiques neoliberalism as an ideology driven by greed and antagonistic competition, showing how it undermines community and co-opts mindfulness for its own ends. He offers the Buddha's teachings on generosity and compassion as the antidote, calling for a fundamental shift in attitude from self-centeredness toward shared joy and care.

relevance.note: Directly examines neoliberalism corrupting Buddhist mindfulness practice
